organization.fullName: University of Trás-os-Montes and Alto Douro

identificationModule.briefTitle: Exercise in Hypoxia and Nitrate Supplementation in Athletes: is the Whole Greater Than the Sum of Its Parts?
identificationModule.officialTitle: Exercise in Hypoxia and Nitrate Supplementation in Athletes: is the Whole Greater Than the Sum of Its Parts?

leadSponsor.name: University of Trás-os-Montes and Alto Douro

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Determine the combined effect of training in hypoxia and nitrate supplementation on the performance and health of athletes at sea-level and altitude conditions.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: Determine the combined effect of training in hypoxia and nitrate supplementation on the performance and health of athletes at sea-level and altitude conditions. It is hypothesized that the combined effect of these interventions will promote an additional performance and health improvement in this population at sea-level and altitude conditions, compared with the single effect.

armGroups.label: HNO: High intensity interval training (HIIT) + nitrate supllementation (NO3-).
armGroups.description: Performed 12 high-intensity interval-training (HIIT) sessions during a 4-week period (3 sessions/ week) under normobaric hypoxia conditions (FiO2=\~13%, \~3000 m) with NO3- supplement.

armGroups.label: HPL: High intensity interval training (HIIT) + placebo supllementation.
armGroups.description: Performed 12 high-intensity interval-training (HIIT) sessions during a 4-week period (3 sessions/ week) under normobaric hypoxia conditions (FiO2=\~13%, \~3000 m) with placebo supplement.

armGroups.label: CON: High intensity interval training (HIIT) + placebo supllementation.
armGroups.description: Performed 12 high-intensity interval-training (HIIT) sessions during a 4-week period (3 sessions/ week) under normoxic conditions (FiO2=\~20.9%) with placebo supplement.

interventions.name: Nitrate + Hypoxia
interventions.description: The combined effect of nitrate supplementation and hypoxia training on both performance and health at sea-level and altitude conditions.
armGroupLabels: HNO: High intensity interval training (HIIT) + nitrate supllementation (NO3-).

interventions.name: Placebo + Hypoxia
interventions.description: The combined effect of placebo supplementation and hypoxia training on both performance and health at sea-level and altitude conditions.
armGroupLabels: HPL: High intensity interval training (HIIT) + placebo supllementation.

interventions.name: Control
interventions.description: The combined effect of placebo supplementation and normoxia training on both performance and health at sea-level and altitude conditions.
armGroupLabels: CON: High intensity interval training (HIIT) + placebo supllementation.

primaryOutcomes.description: VO2 kinetics (time constant and amplitude of fast and slow oxygen components)
Maximal oxygen uptake (VO2max: absolute and relative value)
Lactate threshold (absolute and percentage of VO2max)

Oxidative enzyme activity (cytochrome oxidase subunit IV), citrate synthase, transcription factors (HIF-1alpha and PGC-1alpha) and GLUT4 - assessed through standard enzymatic fluorometric assay and western bllotting
Mitochondrial and capillary density, capillary-to-fibre ratio and fibre cross-section area - cryosections cut with a cryostat and further samples analysed by fluorescence microscopy
Blood pressure (Hmmg), mean arterial pressure (calculated as 1/3 \* systolic pressure + 2/3 \* diastolic pressure)
Plasma \[NO2-\] and \[NO3-\], erythropoietin (EPO), haemoglobin (Hb) and haematocrit (Hct) - all assays on plasma sample analysed by spectrophometry

primaryOutcomes.measure: Oxidative stress and antioxidant markers adaptations
primaryOutcomes.description: Nitrotyrosine, malondialdehyde, ferric-reducing antioxidant power and superoxide dismutase concentrations - all assays on plasma sample analysed by spectrophometry

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Sleep and cardiac autonomic activity adaptations
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Moment #1 (2 weeks pre-intervention - baseline) and moment #2 (4 weeks intervention)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Sleep and cardiac autonomic activity adaptations
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Moment #1 (2 weeks pre-intervention - baseline) and moment #2 (4 weeks intervention)

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* trained male subjects, regularly involved in competitive running, cycling and/ or triathlon events (4-5 times per week) in the past five years

Exclusion Criteria:

* smoking or other chronic issues
* using medication or dietary supplements in the last 3 months, or during the duration of the study
* being exposed to altitude (≥2000 m) in the last 3 months
